text: Moving from the quiet, slightly sterile emptiness of an event space into the stimulating, colorful vibrancy of an exhibit, I am struck by how differently each environment shapes my attention. Alone in the white-walled room, there is a sense of anticipation, neutrality, and openness—space for thought but also for uncertainty. In contrast, the exhibit's bold colors and active displays feel energizing and directed. Here, curiosity blooms as knowledge and creativity are beckoned by the visuals and ideas presented. I feel both contemplative and invigorated by the shift—a reminder of how spaces provoke and sculpt my inner world.
